headline: US Supreme Court backs FCC in clash with wireless carriers over fines
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court backed the Federal Communications Commission's system for levying fines, ruling on Thursday against wireless carriers AT&T and Verizon in their challenge to the agency and handing a win ‌to President Donald Trump's administration.  At issue in the legal dispute was whether the agency's in-house proceedings for ‌imposing the penalties deprived the companies of their right to a jury trial under the U.S. Constitution.  Trump's administration defended the FCC's system for assessing financial penalties, known ​as forfeiture orders.

raw.headline: US Supreme Court backs FCC in clash with wireless carriers over fines
raw.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court backed the Federal Communications Commission's system for levying fines, ruling on Thursday against wireless carriers AT&T and Verizon in their challenge to the agency and handing a win ‌to President Donald Trump's administration.  At issue in the legal dispute was whether the agency's in-house proceedings for ‌imposing the penalties deprived the companies of their right to a jury trial under the U.S. Constitution.  Trump's administration defended the FCC's system for assessing financial penalties, known ​as forfeiture orders.

headline: How to Maximize Dividend Income in Retirement Before RMDs Change the Math
summary: At the 24% federal bracket, a portfolio throwing off $40,000 in high-yield dividend income hands roughly $9,600 to the IRS every year when those shares sit in a taxable account treated as ordinary income. For investors in the gap years between retirement and RMD age 73, that drag compounds quietly until required minimum distributions force ... How to Maximize Dividend Income in Retirement Before RMDs Change the Math

headline: Can Pure Dividend Stocks Replace a $110,000 Dual-Income Household Income? Here’s What It Would Take
summary: A combined household income of $110,000 is close to the national norm for a two-earner household. For a 56-year-old couple hoping to retire at age 60 and fund their lifestyle entirely through dividend income, that annual amount becomes the income target their portfolio must replace. headline: Verizon Confronts Store Backlash And Starlink Threat As Valuation Gap Widens
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) is facing employee pushback after changes to in-store sales policies under its new CEO. Reports highlight customer frustrations tied to these policy shifts, raising questions about the impact on Verizon’s retail experience. A major Wall Street brokerage has also pointed to SpaceX’s Starlink expansion as a potential disruptor to Verizon’s core broadband business. For investors watching NYSE:VZ, these developments sit on top of Verizon’s position as a large US...
